steps.title: Convert the line equation
steps.content: Divide both sides of $2y=12.5$ by $2$: $$y=6.25$$ Since the line intersects the parabola at exactly one point, the resulting quadratic must have exactly one solution.

steps.title: Set the discriminant to zero
steps.content: Set the parabola equal to the line: $$-9x^2+bx=6.25$$ $$9x^2-bx+6.25=0$$ For exactly one solution, the discriminant is $0$: $$(-b)^2-4(9)(6.25)=0$$

steps.title: Solve for the positive constant
steps.content: Simplify the discriminant equation: $$b^2-225=0$$ $$b^2=225$$ Because $b$ is positive, use the positive square root. The answer is 15.
